name: Trace Explorer
description: Distributed tracing viewer with trace search, latency histograms, full waterfall visualization, and service topology mapping.

page_briefs.traces: Trace search landing page. A chart grid at the top renders a latency histogram and error rate distribution across recent traces. Below, a full-width data table lists traces with columns for trace ID, root service, duration, span count, and status, supporting sort, filter, and time range controls.
page_briefs.trace-detail: Full trace waterfall visualization. Each span renders as a horizontal bar proportional to its duration, nested by parent-child relationships. Clicking a span reveals tags, logs, and linked resources in a slide-out panel. Critical path highlighting shows the longest chain of dependent spans.
page_briefs.service-topology: Interactive service dependency graph rendered as a force-directed or hierarchical layout. Nodes represent services sized by request volume; edges show call relationships annotated with latency percentiles and error rates. Hovering a node highlights upstream and downstream dependencies.

directives: Trace explorer KPIs (the kpi-grid on /traces) MUST show trace-specific metrics — average trace duration, p99 latency, error rate, traces/second. Do NOT reuse the metrics dashboard KPIs (requests/sec, SLO budget). Trace explorer is for investigating individual request paths; its KPIs should reflect trace-level aggregates.

Trace data-table columns (in order): Trace ID (mono link) · Root span (METHOD /path) · Service (chip) · Duration · Spans · Status (ok|error pill) · Started (timestamp). Status uses d-annotation with data-status="error|success".
